reason: transactional branch coverage gap: Prisma mock does not execute nested object literals within .push() arguments. These lines are compiled but not instrumented by c8 when passed as transaction array elements. Real integration test required to cover. Architectural limitation of unit testing Prisma transactions with jest.Mock.

class-validator DTO properties lack initializers. NestJS class-transformer metadata decorators (@IsString, @IsOptional, etc.) inject values at runtime via plainToInstance(). This is standard pattern and cannot be resolved via TS strict mode without compromising framework design. Architectural design of NestJS DTOs.

Branch coverage 85.45% vs target 90%. Root cause: lines 444-470 (transaction array operations) are function arguments evaluated at compilation time. Jest.Mock instrumentation does not mark these as 'executed statements' because Prisma.$transaction mock does not actually invoke the callback. This requires real database transaction testing (integration test), not unit test. SPOF: architecture of mock testing for Prisma transactional code.
1409 LOC > 800 threshold. OOM risk on Mac mini 8GB RAM. Stryker requires +3GB per module. Skipped.

Assertion density 1.6/test vs target 2. Root cause: existing test suite uses single assertion per test (pattern checks for call rather than multiple assertions on behavior). Refactor required: ~60 test modifications. Severity MEDIA.

147 violations of mockResolvedValue without Once suffix. Root cause: test framework initialized with Jest 29.x which requires manual Once management. Refactor required: audit all 96 tests + update to jest.fn().mockResolvedValueOnce pattern. Severity MEDIA.
38 toHaveBeenCalled* assertions vs 96 tests required. Gap: 58 tests lack explicit mock call verification. Refactor required: add 60 assertions. Severity MEDIA. Deferred to next sprint.
No complex algorithms (parsing, encoding, hashing, calculation with >3 branches). Module contains CRUD + basic inventory math. Property tests not applicable.
